market.advice: SPY is below its 50-day MA and down 2.4% this week, but don't panic: QQQ is holding above its 50-day, breadth is healthy at 66% of stocks above their 50-day MAs, and VIX is at 18.3 — falling, not spiking. Two distribution days registered, no follow-through yet. The US-Iran ceasefire overnight removes a key geopolitical tail risk that had been weighing on energy and sentiment. The action plan: trade smaller size, hold only your strongest names, and use this consolidation to build your watchlist. Only A+ setups. Focus on stocks whose RS lines are at new highs — those are the real leaders regardless of what the index does.

market_news.title: Stocks perk up and oil cools as US and Iran halt hostilities
market_news.summary: US equities rallied and crude oil pulled back after Washington and Tehran agreed to a halt in hostilities, removing a near-term geopolitical risk that had been pressuring risk assets and lifting energy prices. The ceasefire signals a de-escalation that could sustain the recent relief rally in equities.

market_news.title: Everything tied to the data center is suddenly suspect. Can Big Tech fix it?
market_news.summary: Concerns mount over the sustainability of hyperscaler AI capex after several large data center operators flagged demand softness. The narrative shift is weighing on semis and cloud names that had priced in an uninterrupted build-out cycle, creating a near-term headwind for AI infrastructure plays.

market_news.title: Here are 3 big things we're watching in the stock market in the week ahead
market_news.summary: Key themes for this week: the June jobs report (Friday), Fed-speak on rate path after recent inflation data, and whether the broad market can stage a follow-through day after two distribution days on the S&P 500. A strong jobs number could reprice rate cut expectations lower.

market_news.title: Gold falls as Mideast tensions ease; Fed rate-hike bets back in focus
market_news.summary: Gold reversed sharply on the Iran-US ceasefire news as the safe-haven premium unwound. Attention pivots back to the Fed rate path; stronger-than-expected recent inflation data had briefly re-priced rate hike odds higher, which is a headwind for rate-sensitive equities and growth multiples.

market_news.title: Asia shares: South Korea chip push steadies semiconductor sentiment
market_news.summary: Asian markets were mixed but the semiconductor complex found a floor as South Korea's government announced additional support for domestic chip capacity, steadying sentiment around memory and logic chip names heading into the US open. HBM demand outlook remains a key debate.
